item.title: Insmed's Brinsupri (Brensocatib)
item.description:
• Summary: Insmed's drug Brinsupri (brensocatib) has failed in a mid-stage study for hidradenitis suppurativa, leading the company to shelve plans in this skin disease. The drug, a dipeptidyl peptidase 1 inhibitor, did not meet efficacy endpoints, prompting a refocus on its core development in chronic lung conditions like bronchiectasis.
  • MoAs: Brensocatib is a dipeptidyl peptidase 1 (DPP1) inhibitor, which targets neutrophil serine proteases to reduce inflammation.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, BioSpace, Endpoints News

item.title: Johnson & Johnson's Tremfya (Guselkumab)
item.description:
• Summary: Johnson & Johnson's Tremfya (guselkumab) has retaken the crown for highest TV ad spending among drugs, outpacing competitors like AbbVie's Humira. The IL-23 inhibitor, used for psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis, is being aggressively marketed to maintain its position in the competitive immunology market.
  • MoAs: Guselkumab is a monoclonal antibody that selectively inhibits interleukin-23 (IL-23), a cytokine involved in inflammatory and immune responses.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma
